perturbation.original_text: Each party, as a Receiving Party, agrees that it will: (a) not use, or authorize the use of, such Confidential Information for any purpose other than for the evaluation, negotiation and, if applicable, consummation of the Potential Transaction; (b) other than as permitted by Section 4 of this Agreement, hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect such Confidential Information with the same degree of care (but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care) such party normally uses to protect its own information that is similar in type or nature to the Confidential Information; (c) not disclose such Confidential Information to any person other than to those of its and/or its affiliated companies’ respective employees, stockholders, partners, members, directors, officers, advisors, agents, accountants, attorneys, representatives and/or consultants (collectively, “Representatives” ), in each case, who (i) need to know such Confidential Information to facilitate the evaluation, negotiation and, if applicable, consummation of the Potential Transaction, and (ii) are advised of the confidential and proprietary nature of such Confidential Information and are bound by confidentiality CONFIDENTIAL 2obligations (which may be contained in such Representatives’ engagement agreements) that limit the further use and disclosure of such Confidential Information; (d) not copy or reproduce all or any part of such Confidential Information in any medium, except as may be strictly necessary to facilitate the use of Confidential Information as permitted by this Agreement; and (e) not decompile, disassemble or reverse engineer all or any part of such Confidential Information. For the avoidance of doubt, any use of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information by any Representative of the Receiving Party must be in strict accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Without limiting any obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breaches of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party, as a Receiving Party, agrees that it will: (a) not use, or authorize the use of, such Confidential Information for any purpose other than for the evaluation, negotiation and, if applicable, consummation of the Potential Transaction; (b) other than as permitted by Section 4 of this Agreement, hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect such Confidential Information with the same degree of care (but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care) such party normally uses to protect its own information that is similar in type or nature to the Confidential Information; (c) not disclose such Confidential Information to any person other than to those of its and/or its affiliated companies’ respective employees, stockholders, partners, members, directors, officers, advisors, agents, accountants, attorneys, representatives and/or consultants (collectively, “Representatives” ), in each case, who (i) need to know such Confidential Information to facilitate the evaluation, negotiation and, if applicable, consummation of the Potential Transaction, and (ii) are advised of the confidential and proprietary nature of such Confidential Information and are bound by confidentiality obligations (which may be contained in such Representatives’ engagement agreements) that limit the further use and disclosure of such Confidential Information; (d) not copy or reproduce all or any part of such Confidential Information in any medium, except as may be strictly necessary to facilitate the use of Confidential Materials as permitted by this Agreement; and (e) not decompile, disassemble or reverse engineer all or any part of such Confidential Information. For the avoidance of doubt, any use of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information by any Representative of the Receiving Party must be in strict accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Without limiting any obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breaches of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Information” to “Confidential Materials” in subsection (d) creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement defines Confidential Information. By switching to Confidential Materials, it's unclear whether the restriction on copying/reproducing applies only to a subset of the defined “Confidential Information,” or introduces a new undefined term with an unclear scope. This contradicts with the definition of the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party, as a Receiving Party, agrees that it will: (a) not use, or authorize the use of, such Confidential Information for any purpose other than for the evaluation, negotiation and, if applicable, consummation of the Potential Transaction; (b) other than as permitted by Section 4 of this Agreement, hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect such Confidential Information with the same degree of care (but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care) such party normally uses to protect its own information that is similar in type or nature to the Confidential Information; (c) not disclose such Confidential Information to any person other than to those of its and/or its affiliated companies’ respective employees, stockholders, partners, members, directors, officers, advisors, agents, accountants, attorneys, representatives and/or consultants (collectively, “Representatives” ), in each case, who (i) need to know such Confidential Information to facilitate the evaluation, negotiation and, if applicable, consummation of the Potential Transaction, and (ii) are advised of the confidential and proprietary nature of such Confidential Information and are bound by confidentiality obligations (which may be contained in such Representatives’ engagement agreements) that limit the further use and disclosure of such Confidential Information; (d) not copy or reproduce all or any part of such Confidential Information in any medium, except as may be strictly necessary to facilitate the use of Confidential Materials as permitted by this Agreement; and (e) not decompile, disassemble or reverse engineer all or any part of such Confidential Information. For the avoidance of doubt, any use of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information by any Representative of the Receiving Party must be in strict accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Without limiting any obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breaches of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies subsection (d) by replacing "Confidential Information" with "Confidential Materials." The rest of the section refers to "Confidential Information." This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement defines Confidential Information, and by introducing "Confidential Materials" without defining it, it's unclear whether the restriction on copying/reproducing applies only to a subset of the defined "Confidential Information," or introduces a new undefined term with an unclear scope. This contradicts with the overall intent of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Provided that each party complies with its obligations contained herein, and except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement shall not in any way limit, restrict or preclude either party from pursuing any of its present or future business activities or interests or from entering into any agreement or transaction with any person, regardless of whether such business activities or interests are competitive with the business activities and interests of the other party and regardless of whether the subject matter of any such agreement or transaction is in any way similar to or different from the transactions considered and evaluated by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Provided that each party complies with its obligations contained herein, and except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement shall not in any way limit, restrict or preclude either party from pursuing any of its present or future business activities or interests or from entering into any agreement or transaction with any individual, regardless of whether such business activities or interests are competitive with the business activities and interests of the other party and regardless of whether the subject matter of any such agreement or transaction is in any way similar to or different from the transactions considered and evaluated by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “person” to “individual” creates ambiguity. 'Person' is defined broadly in Section 21(f) to include corporations, companies, etc. By using 'individual' in Section 7, it could be argued that the freedom to pursue competitive activities only applies to agreements with natural persons and not corporate entities, contradicting the broad definition provided and limiting the scope of permitted activities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) For purposes of this Agreement, the term “person” shall be broadly interpreted to include without limitation any corporation, company, group, partnership and natural person.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'person' to 'individual'. The contradicted text defines 'person' to include corporations, companies, groups, partnerships and natural persons. By changing 'person' to 'individual', it implies that the allowance for competitive activities only applies to agreements with natural persons, contradicting the broader definition of 'person' elsewhere in the contract and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All actions or proceedings arising in connection with, touching upon or relating to this Agreement, the breach thereof and/or the scope of the provisions of this Section 19 other than actions or proceedings seeking equitable relief (a “Proceeding” ) shall be submitted to JAMS ( "JAMS" ) for binding arbitration CONFIDENTIAL 5under its Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures if the matter in dispute is over $250,000 or under its Streamlined Arbitration Rules and Procedures if the matter in dispute is $250,000 or less (as applicable, the “Rules” ) to be held solely in Los Angeles County, California, U.S.A., in the English language in accordance with the provisions below.
perturbation.changed_text: All actions or proceedings arising in connection with, touching upon or relating to this Agreement, the breach thereof and/or the scope of the provisions of this Section 19 other than actions or proceedings seeking equitable relief (a “Proceeding”, also known as "Legal Action" ) shall be submitted to JAMS ( "JAMS" ) for binding arbitration CONFIDENTIAL 5under its Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures if the matter in dispute is over $250,000 or under its Streamlined Arbitration Rules and Procedures if the matter in dispute is $250,000 or less (as applicable, the “Rules” ) to be held solely in Los Angeles County, California, U.S.A., in the English language in accordance with the provisions below.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'also known as "Legal Action"' to the definition of "Proceeding" introduces a contradiction if "Legal Action" is interpreted to include actions outside of arbitration. Later references to "Proceeding" may be challenged if a party initiates a court action, claiming it's a "Legal Action" not subject to the arbitration clause. This is contradicted due to the in-text addition of new term which is not defined in the context and contradicts the meaning of proceeding
perturbation.contradicted_text: All actions or proceedings arising in connection with, touching upon or relating to this Agreement, the breach thereof and/or the scope of the provisions of this Section 19 other than actions or proceedings seeking equitable relief (a “Proceeding”, also known as "Legal Action" ) shall be submitted to JAMS ( "JAMS" ) for binding arbitration CONFIDENTIAL 5under its Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures if the matter in dispute is over $250,000 or under its Streamlined Arbitration Rules and Procedures if the matter in dispute is $250,000 or less (as applicable, the “Rules” ) to be held solely in Los Angeles County, California, U.S.A., in the English language in accordance with the provisions below.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'also known as "Legal Action"' to the definition of "Proceeding" in the changed text creates a contradiction because "Legal Action" is not explicitly defined within the context of the agreement. This introduces ambiguity, allowing a party to argue that 'Legal Action' encompasses actions outside the intended scope of the arbitration clause, which is to submit all 'Proceedings' to JAMS for binding arbitration. The contradicted text repeats this changed definition, therefore the contradiction is present. The change allows for potential court actions, which contradicts the arbitration clause.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, in connection with any claim of breach hereunder, each party hereby irrevocably waives any right or remedy to seek and/or obtain injunctive or other equitable relief or any order with respect to, and/or to enjoin or restrain or otherwise impair in any manner, the production, distribution, exhibition or other exploitation of CONFIDENTIAL 6any motion picture, television or other audio-visual production related to the other party, its parents, subsidiaries and affiliates, or the use, publication or dissemination of any advertising in connection with such production; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not prohibit a party from seeking or obtaining injunctive or other equitable relief or any order based on a claim that such production uses the other party’s Confidential Information to infringe upon such party's intellectual property rights.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, in connection with any claim of breach hereunder, each party hereby irrevocably waives any right or remedy to seek and/or obtain injunctive or other equitable relief or any order with respect to, and/or to enjoin or restrain or otherwise impair in any manner, the production, distribution, exhibition or other exploitation of CONFIDENTIAL 6any motion picture, television or other audio-visual production related to the other party, its parents, subsidiaries and affiliates, or the use, publication or dissemination of any advertising in connection with such Content; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not prohibit a party from seeking or obtaining injunctive or other equitable relief or any order based on a claim that such production uses the other party’s Confidential Information to infringe upon such party's intellectual property rights.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'advertising in connection with such production' to 'advertising in connection with such Content' in Section 19 creates a contradiction. 'Content' is not defined in the agreement. This raises questions whether the waiver applies only to advertising directly related to the specific 'production' or extends to all 'Content' the other party produces, potentially affecting remedies for breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, in connection with any claim of breach hereunder, each party hereby irrevocably waives any right or remedy to seek and/or obtain injunctive or other equitable relief or any order with respect to, and/or to enjoin or restrain or otherwise impair in any manner, the production, distribution, exhibition or other exploitation of CONFIDENTIAL 6any motion picture, television or other audio-visual production related to the other party, its parents, subsidiaries and affiliates, or the use, publication or dissemination of any advertising in connection with such Content; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not prohibit a party from seeking or obtaining injunctive or other equitable relief or any order based on a claim that such production uses the other party’s Confidential Information to infringe upon such party's intellectual property rights.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'advertising in connection with such production,' which is more specific. The changed text broadens this to 'advertising in connection with such Content.' Since 'Content' is not defined and the contradiction explanation clarifies this introduces uncertainty about the scope of the waiver, a contradiction exists because it is unclear if the waiver applies only to advertising directly related to the specific 'production' or extends to all 'Content' the other party produces. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, creating conflicting obligations because the scope of 'Content' is ambiguous and undefined relative to the explicit term 'production'. The contradicted text is the changed text itself.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding any other provision hereof, including Section 21(c), the parties agree that this Agreement shall apply only with respect to the Potential Transaction, and Confidential Information received and discussions occurring in connection therewith, and shall not apply to any confidential information that may be exchanged or discussions occurring in connection with any Other Agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any other provision hereof, including Section 21(c), the parties agree that this Agreement shall apply only with respect to the Potential Transaction, and Confidential Information received and discussions occurring in connection therewith, and shall not apply to any company secrets that may be exchanged or discussions occurring in connection with any Other Agreements.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “confidential information” to “company secrets” creates an in-text contradiction. 'Confidential Information' is defined in Section 1, while 'company secrets' is a new undefined term. This makes it unclear whether information exchanged under “Other Agreements” is protected under the same terms as 'Confidential Information' or subject to a different standard, potentially weakening the protections for such information and conflicting with Section 1's broad definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” shall mean all information disclosed, directly or indirectly, through any means of communication or observation, by or on behalf of one party hereto (the "Disclosing Party" ) to the other party hereto (the "Receiving Party" ) on or after the date hereof, that relates to or is derived from the Disclosing Party’s business, strategic, marketing, technological or creative affairs, or to any other matter that the Receiving Party is advised or has reason to know is the confidential or proprietary information of the Disclosing Party. Any material provided by either party to the other which is clearly designated "Confidential" (or other similar legend) will be presumed to be Confidential Information; the absence of any such legend, however, will not preclude the same from being deemed Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the defined term 'Confidential Information' with the undefined term 'company secrets'. This creates a contradiction because the original agreement explicitly defines 'Confidential Information' in relation to the potential transaction, while the altered language introduces a new, undefined term regarding other agreements. This new term is not beholden to the definitions set in the 'Contradicted Text', creating an in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
